title: Forcepoint Remote Browser Isolation Session Audit (`forcepoint:one`)
dataSources: Forcepoint cloud SSE (`forcepoint:one`), on-prem Web Security (`websense:cg:kv`), Email (`forcepoint:email:kv`), DLP (`forcepoint:dlp`)
app: Forcepoint Insights SIEM App (Splunkbase 8053); Forcepoint Web Security TA (`websense:cg:kv`); Email Security KV (`forcepoint:email:kv`)
spl:
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(event_type),"(?i)isolation|rbi|remote browser")
| stats count by user, url_host, action
| sort -count
description: Isolation contains risky clicks; auditing sessions proves the control ran for regulated user populations.
value: Security teams audit Remote Browser Isolation session patterns to measure how effectively RBI protects users from risky web content, quantify blocked downloads and clipboard transfers, and identify users who frequently trigger isolation policies.
implementation: Install the Insights SIEM app, configure HEC inputs per product line, and align indexes (`proxy`, `email`, `dlp`, `sase`). Normalize usernames to enterprise SAMAccountName.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Email.Email
  by Email.user, Email.action, Email.src, Email.dest span=1h
| sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Forcepoint Web Security

knownFalsePositives: Spikes from new SaaS rollouts, automated workflows, sanctioned bulk uploads, or M&A integration.
grandmaExplanation: We keep track of when our internet safety system opens websites in a protected bubble instead of directly on someone's computer, which keeps them safe from harmful content.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Forcepoint Insights SIEM App (Splunkbase 8053) or direct integration from Forcepoint ONE SSE.
• Data in `index=sase` with `sourcetype=forcepoint:one`. Key fields: `event_type` (isolation), `user`, `url`, `category`, `isolation_reason` (policy/risk/uncategorized), `session_duration_sec`, `render_mode` (read_only/full_interaction), `file_download_blocked`, `clipboard_blocked`.
• Forcepoint ONE SSE with Remote Browser Isolation (RBI) module enabled. RBI renders web content in a cloud sandbox, streaming only pixels to the user — protecting against zero-day web exploits.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify RBI session events:
```spl
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(event_type), "isolation|rbi")
| stats count dc(user) as users by isolation_reason, render_mode
```
If empty: RBI may not be enabled in the policy, or the event type naming may differ. Check `| stats count by event_type` for the actual event type values.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — RBI session audit:
```spl
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(event_type), "isolation|rbi")
| stats count as sessions avg(session_duration_sec) as avg_duration_sec dc(url) as unique_urls sum(eval(if(file_download_blocked="true", 1, 0))) as downloads_blocked by user, isolation_reason
| eval avg_duration_min=round(avg_duration_sec/60, 1)
| sort - sessions
```

Understanding this SPL: RBI sessions are triggered by policy (risky categories, uncategorized sites, or specific URL patterns). Users with many RBI sessions may be frequently visiting risky sites. `downloads_blocked` shows how many times RBI prevented file downloads from isolated sites — each blocked download is a potential malware delivery prevented. Long average session durations suggest users are actively working in isolated contexts, which may indicate a need to reclassify the site.

RBI effectiveness:
```spl
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-7d
| where match(lower(event_type), "isolation|rbi")
| stats count as total_sessions sum(eval(if(file_download_blocked="true", 1, 0))) as downloads_blocked sum(eval(if(clipboard_blocked="true", 1, 0))) as clipboard_blocked by category
| eval protection_value=downloads_blocked + clipboard_blocked
| sort - protection_value
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In Forcepoint ONE portal: RBI > Session Reports. Compare session counts with Splunk.
(b) Navigate to a site that triggers RBI (e.g., an uncategorized site). Verify the session appears in Splunk.
(c) Attempt a file download from an isolated site. Verify `file_download_blocked=true` appears in Splunk.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard ("Forcepoint — Browser Isolation"):
• Row 1 — Single-value: "RBI sessions (24h)", "Downloads blocked", "Clipboard transfers blocked".
• Row 2 — Bar chart: sessions by isolation reason.
• Row 3 — Table: top users, session count, unique URLs, blocked actions.

Alert: Weekly report on RBI usage. Trigger alert when a single user exceeds 50 RBI sessions in 24h (excessive risky browsing).

Runbook:
1. **User with excessive RBI sessions** — Review the URLs. If legitimate work sites: consider reclassifying or adding to an allowlist.
2. **Many downloads blocked** — RBI is protecting against potential malware. Investigate the source URLs.
3. **RBI sessions declining** — Users may be bypassing the proxy. Check agent enrollment (UC-17.5.13).

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No RBI events** — The RBI module may not be licensed or enabled. Check Forcepoint ONE > Settings > Modules.
• **Session duration always zero** — The event may capture only the start, not the end. Check if session-end events are a separate event type.
• **Users complaining about RBI performance** — RBI adds latency due to cloud rendering. Monitor `session_duration_sec` — if much higher than the same site without RBI, consider adding the site to the direct access list.
